issues.title: EXP-450 P2: EM-incorrect 1/r decay claim in 2.14 ring-magnet subsection
issues.description: The proposed 2.14 ring-magnet subsection third paragraph asserts "1/r-type decay (or 1/r² in spherically symmetric cases)" — this is wrong. Magnetic dipole far-field decays as 1/r³. Dome defenders can use this to discredit the entire proposal. Fix: delete the third paragraph entirely; paragraphs 1-2 stand on their own.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete P2 third paragraph ("The exponential form is also incompatible..."). The flux-conservation argument in paragraphs 1-2 is the strongest version of WIN-053. If a decay argument is desired, use 1/r³ dipole far-field.

issues.description: P4 (promote §2.9 to §2.1) underspecifies the cross-reference burden: 13 anchor refs (#2-9) and 14 prose refs (Section 2.9) across all parts. Of these, Part 1 alone has 3 anchor refs affecting build-scripts/generate-html.js. P4 operations need a Step 5 sweep list or the decider must track all renaming sites.
issues.suggested_fix: Add Step 5 to P4.operations: sweep all "Section 2.X" prose references and "#2-X" anchor references. Inventory first: grep -oE "Section 2.[1-9] |#2-[1-9] " data/sections.json. Alternative: use italic-emphasis on existing §2.9 title instead of renumbering.
issues.routing_reason: BAU priority3b: major/critical severity routed to analyst for EXP authoring

issues.title: EXP-450 P6: "in Part 2" count scope mismatch — self-contradictions not all in Part 2
issues.description: P6 proposed insertion reads "any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model..." but {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} resolves to 20 (all review self-contradictions), while only a subset are in Part 2. Fix: change "in Part 2" to "identified in this review".
issues.suggested_fix: Change P6 insertion to: "any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions identified in this review alone falsifies the model..." — scope now matches the data.
issues.routing_reason: BAU priority3b: major/critical severity routed to analyst for EXP authoring

issues.title: EXP-450 P1: 2.13 κ divide-by-zero metaphor is literal, not pedagogical
issues.description: P1 2.13 subsection headline uses "treating it as 1.67 nT/µGal is treating 1/0 as a finite number" — this is a compression metaphor that dome defenders can attack as technically inaccurate. The actual argument is that the coupling constant's denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision data.
issues.suggested_fix: Soften to: "...A coupling constant whose denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision confirmations cannot be treated as a fixed structural coefficient at the precision the dome itself cites." Remove "treating 1/0 as a finite number" framing.

issues.title: EXP-450 P5: §1.6 Dielectric Foundation dropped from Tier-1 expansion without rationale
issues.description: EXP-450 P5 expands Tier-1 framing to sections 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 but the parent HOL-HIERARCHY c2 review named 1.5/1.6/1.7/1.8/1.9 (five sections including 1.6). Dropping §1.6 without explanation will cause the next curmudgeon review to flag the omission.
issues.suggested_fix: Add exclusion_rationale to P5: "Section 1.6 (Dielectric Foundation) is excluded because it critiques dome interpretation of real observations (Tier-2-style) rather than attacking the dome framework using dome-published parameters (Tier-1-style)."

issues.title: EXP-450 P0 op4: Summary paste anchor misplaced — inside cosmological-cluster closing div
issues.description: P0 operation 4 instructs pasting the Summary BEFORE the closing <!-- ═══ Part 2b ═══ --> comment, but that comment is INSIDE the cosmological-cluster's closing </details> tag at sections.json:part2.html offset 58,682. The Summary would be placed mid-structure.
issues.suggested_fix: Tighten P0 operation 4: append Summary at end-of-string in part2.html (after cosmological-cluster closing </details>); relocate <!-- ═══ Part 2b ═══ --> marker to follow the Summary block.

issues.title: EXP-450: P4 sequencing vs P0-P3 conflict — P4 must land before P0-P3
issues.description: EXP-450 sequencing_note says P4 (V13 promotion) must land BEFORE P0-P3 (which anchor on the post-Summary §2.10-2.12 positions). But recommended_decider_action says push P0+P1+P2+P3 as default bundle without mentioning this dependency.
issues.suggested_fix: Add sequencing contingency: (1) push P0+P1+P2+P3+P5+P6 as default bundle (class=holistic). (2) Open operator note for P4 with cross-reference burden estimate (~27 sites). If P4 accepted, land P4 first then P0-P3.

issues.title: EXP-451 P2: 22.6 Hz worked example misattributes source — should be 8.77 Hz pole-floor or name averaging explicitly
issues.description: P2 worked-example says dome predicts 22.6 Hz from "flat-plate resonator with published exponentially-decaying firmament profile." But existing review (line 1063) attributes 22.6 Hz to the linear radial average H_eff = H₀·(L/R)·(1−exp(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km — an averaging convention. H(0) = 8,537 km gives 8.77 Hz (the unambiguous pole-floor). Using 22.6 Hz without naming the averaging convention mirrors the dome's own formula-switching behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Option A (cleaner): use H(0) = 8,537 km → f = c/(4H) = 8.77 Hz, no averaging needed. Option B: keep 22.6 Hz but explicitly name the integration scheme: "the linear-radial-averaged effective height H_eff ≈ 3,316 km in the quarter-wave formula".

issues.title: EXP-451 P3b: aria-label not updated alongside visible span text
issues.description: P3b updates visible <span class="ds-fm-status-sub"> text to add ~1 nT discriminating window but does NOT update the surrounding role="status" aria-label which still reads the old wording. Screen-reader users miss the ~1 nT detail.
issues.location: docs/index.html line 446 (ds-fm-status row, C2 eclipse), EXP-451 P3b
issues.suggested_fix: Add sub-patch to P3b: update aria-label on the same div to include "~1 nT discriminating window" detail.

issues.title: EXP-451 P3a: "We will upgrade" conditional language weaker than existing sufficiency assertion
issues.description: New caption "We will upgrade the relevant verdict if any one is met" introduces opt-out room. Previous wording was more definitive ("any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict"). A dome defender can challenge "if any one is met" as conditional promise rather than firm commitment.
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: change to "Any one is sufficient to upgrade the relevant verdict" to retain the sufficiency assertion while keeping the pre-commitment framing.

issues.title: WIN-029 tldr discrepancy: tldr_evidence lists "5.3%, 10.8%, 6.4%" but tldr_verdict lists "5.3%, 6.4%, 12.1%"
issues.description: WIN-029.c5 analyst-baby notes flagged this as a pre-existing internal contradiction. tldr_evidence says error percentages are 5.3%, 10.8%, and 6.4%; tldr_verdict says 5.3%, 6.4%, and 12.1%. Direct inconsistency undermines credibility — a dome defender reading both panels sees different numbers.
issues.suggested_fix: Verify correct percentages against WIN-029 detail_evidence (which has the full analysis). Patch whichever tldr field is wrong to match the detail. Both should quote the same three error values.

issues.title: PROP-059 Mech-A-bypass: 4 ISS closures missing verification_pattern
issues.description: ISS-2295 (fixed_by:decider-prop059-verification), ISS-2296 (fixed_by:decider-self-verify), ISS-2297 (fixed_by:decider-self-verify), ISS-2298 (fixed_by:decider-dedup-step) were all closed on 2026-05-30 without recording verification_pattern fingerprints, post PROP-059 cutoff. Also: ISS-2296 fix set next_id=475 while live items already reached 478 (incomplete fix — now corrected to 479 this run). Each closure needs a verification_pattern grep/node command added confirming the fix is present.
issues.suggested_fix: For each of ISS-2295/2296/2297/2298: (1) re-verify the target is actually fixed at HEAD, (2) add verification_pattern field with a grep/node command that confirms the fix. For ISS-2296 specifically: next_id is now corrected to 479 (done this run). Add audit_grandfathered field only where confirmed correct out-of-band.

issues.title: WIN-023: Add t=0 anchor looseness clause to defend against "5% miss" objection
issues.description: WIN-023 verdict dismisses theological link as unfalsifiable but does not say WHY the 1000-year target band is loose. A close reader could counter "Revelation 20:7 says 1000 years and SAA formed at 950 AD — that is a 5% miss, why not impressive?" The answer (t=0 has multiple candidate starts — resurrection AD33, Constantine AD312, Theodosius AD380 → band AD1033-1380, ~350yr wide) is not stated. Adding one sentence hardens the unfalsifiability verdict.

issues.title: EXP-452: P1 pending operator-apply (generate-html.js) — transient narrative-vs-data cluster gap
issues.description: P2 live (4 cluster detail_group tags in data) but P1 still pending operator-apply (ISS-2341, generate-html.js). Reader reaching Schumann/cosmological/H(r) WINs sees detail_group labels without P1's companion prose explaining the cluster concept. Transient by design — closes when P1 lands. Flag to track.

issues.title: EXP-452 P2: Part 1b magnetic-content breakdown still lists WIN-061 (tension with Schumann cluster taxonomy)
issues.description: Part 1b "New Content Breakdown" classifies WIN-059/060/061 together as "Re-sliced geomagnetic data (WIN-040 through WIN-043, WIN-053, WIN-059-061, WIN-063): 9 WINs." After P2 retaxonomizes WIN-061 as Schumann, this narration is in tension with our cluster taxonomy. Existed before P2 (dome's own V51.0 bundling) but is now sharper. Recommendation: when P1 lands, add gloss to part1b noting WIN-061 is taxonomically Schumann in our cluster classification.

issues.title: WIN-003: detail_evidence still asserts acoustic eigenmodes from chamber dimensions (c2 H1 carry-over)
issues.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 H1, still open in c3: detail_evidence asserts acoustic eigenmodes are determined by chamber/sarcophagus dimensions, but does not account for the dome author's specific counter that the dome's model predicts resonance from the firmament cavity geometry, not the room. The carry-over means c2's recommendation was not applied.

issues.title: WIN-003: detail_evidence checks only 10×7.83=78.3 Hz against 117 Hz observation (c2 H3 carry-over)
issues.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 H3, still open in c3: detail_evidence only checks the 10th harmonic (10×7.83=78.3 Hz) against the 117 Hz observation, without noting that 15×7.83=117.45 Hz is within instrument resolution. The 15th harmonic match is the salient counter — it shows any low-frequency electromagnetic resonance produces integer multiples hitting 117 Hz.

issues.title: WIN-003: code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard=false is incorrect — dome explicitly relabels Schumann resonance as firmament resonance
issues.description: INTERNAL TENSION in c3 review: code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard is currently false in wins.json, but the c3 review notes the dome explicitly relabels standard Schumann resonance physics as firmament resonance. relabels_standard should be true (same as WIN-002/029/038/061 which all use relabels_standard=true for the same reason). The c3 review flags this as a new hole not present in c2.

issues.title: WIN-066: tldr_verdict says "200–800×" while detail_verdict_text says different range
issues.description: NEW (c5 missed): TLDR-vs-detail magnitude-ratio inconsistency. tldr_verdict says "200–800× the actual value" while detail_verdict_text states a different ratio range. These should be consistent.

issues.title: WIN-066: Counter (1) defender-pivot appeals to globe-physics mechanism without stating why it applies (c5 H1 carry-forward)
issues.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #1: Counter (1) of the defender-pivot paragraph appeals to globe-physics mechanisms without providing the specific mechanical link that explains why globe physics produces the observed pattern. The defense survives without this but is weaker than necessary.

issues.title: WIN-066: Counter (2) asserts dome cites +0.34 W/m²/decade but source not specified (c5 H2 carry-forward)
issues.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #2: Counter (2) asserts "The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m²/decade)" but does not specify which dome file or version this appears in, making it hard to verify.

issues.title: WIN-066: Cross-WIN sign-only systemic flag — sign-match non-discrimination (c5 H4 carry-forward, no action until scope clear)
issues.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #4 (still applies, no action): Cross-WIN sign-only systemic flag — sign-match-non-discrimination pattern applies to WIN-066 as a systemic observation. Carry-forward note; no specific patch needed until scope is clarified across other sign-match WINs.
